message:
Fix critical bugs in ru script

1. CRITICAL: Add missing main "$@" call at end of script
   - Script was defining main() but never calling it
   - Script would do nothing when run

2. CRITICAL: Remove duplicate function definitions
   - get_repo_log_path() was defined twice (lines 178 and 264)
   - get_run_log_path() was defined twice
   - update_latest_symlink() was defined twice
   - Removed second set of definitions to avoid confusion

3. FIX: Make sed -i portable for macOS
   - macOS sed requires -i '' while GNU sed uses -i
   - Added OSTYPE check in set_config_value()

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-14","title":"Phase 14: CI/CD Workflows","description":"**EPIC: GitHub Actions CI/CD Workflows**

## Goal
Implement CI workflows for quality assurance and release automation.

## Rationale
Automated testing catches issues before they reach users. ShellCheck finds common shell scripting errors. Behavioral tests verify actual functionality. Release automation ensures consistent, secure distribution.

## CI Workflow (ci.yml)
- ShellCheck on all .sh files
- Bash syntax validation (bash -n)
- Installation test on Ubuntu and macOS
- URL parsing tests
- Local git operation tests
- JSON output validation
- Version consistency check

## Release Workflow (release.yml)
- Triggered on version tags (v*)
- Creates GitHub Release
- Generates SHA256 checksums
- Uploads ru and install.sh as release assets

## Success Criteria
- PRs are blocked on CI failure
- Releases are automated and include checksums
- Tests actually catch real bugs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.540476483-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.569867903-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.598982951-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1401","title":"Create .github/workflows/ci.yml","description":"**Create CI workflow for PRs and pushes**

## What
GitHub Actions workflow for continuous integration.

## Why
Automated testing catches bugs before they reach users.

## Jobs
1. **shellcheck**: Lint all shell scripts
2. **syntax-check**: Bash syntax validation
3. **install-test**: Test installer on Ubuntu and macOS
4. **behavioral-tests**: Run URL parsing and git operation tests
5. **version-consistency**: Verify VERSION file matches script

## Implementation
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  shellcheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
        with:
          severity: warning

  syntax-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: bash -n ru
      - run: bash -n install.sh

  # ... more jobs
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All jobs run on PRs
- ShellCheck catches issues
- Tests actually test functionality","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1401","depends_on_id":"bd-14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.910288124-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1402","title":"Create .github/workflows/release.yml","description":"**Create release workflow**

## What
GitHub Actions workflow for automated releases.

## Why
Automated releases ensure consistent, secure distribution.

## Trigger
On push of version tags: v*

## Steps
1. Validate tag matches VERSION file
2. Create GitHub Release
3. Generate SHA256 checksums
4. Upload ru and install.sh as assets
5. Upload checksums

## Implementation
```yaml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Verify version consistency
        run: |
          TAG_VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
          FILE_VERSION=$(cat VERSION)
          if [[ "$TAG_VERSION" != "$FILE_VERSION" ]]; then
            echo "Version mismatch: tag=$TAG_VERSION file=$FILE_VERSION"
            exit 1
          fi
      
      - name: Generate checksums
        run: |
          sha256sum ru > ru.sha256
          sha256sum install.sh > install.sh.sha256
      
      - name: Create Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          files: |
            ru
            ru.sha256
            install.sh
            install.sh.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Releases created on tag push
- Checksums included
- Version consistency enforced","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1402","depends_on_id":"bd-1401","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.940855899-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1403","title":"Create scripts/test_parsing.sh","description":"**Implement URL parsing tests**

## What
Test script that validates URL parsing functions.

## Why
Parsing is critical infrastructure. Bugs here break everything.

## Test Structure
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SOURCE_DIR="$(dirname "$0")/.."
source "$SOURCE_DIR/ru"

FAILED=0

assert_parses() {
    local url="$1" expected_host="$2" expected_owner="$3" expected_repo="$4"
    local host owner repo
    
    if parse_repo_url "$url" host owner repo; then
        if [[ "$host" != "$expected_host" || "$owner" != "$expected_owner" || "$repo" != "$expected_repo" ]]; then
            echo "FAIL: $url parsed to $host/$owner/$repo (expected $expected_host/$expected_owner/$expected_repo)"
            ((FAILED++))
        else
            echo "PASS: $url"
        fi
    else
        echo "FAIL: $url failed to parse"
        ((FAILED++))
    fi
}

# Test cases
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

exit $FAILED
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All URL formats tested
- Clear pass/fail output
- Returns non-zero on any failure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1403","depends_on_id":"bd-706","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.975042544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1404","title":"Create scripts/test_local_git.sh","description":"**Implement local git operation tests**

## What
Test script that validates git operations without network.

## Why
Git operations must work correctly. Network-free tests are fast and reliable in CI.

## Test Scenarios
1. Status detection: current, behind, ahead, diverged
2. Dirty detection
3. Clone operation
4. Pull operation
5. Remote mismatch detection

## Implementation
Create temporary git repos, simulate various states, verify detection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All status types tested
- Works without network
- Cleans up after itself","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1404","depends_on_id":"bd-808","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.005730076-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15","title":"Phase 15: Testing & Polish","description":"**EPIC: Final Testing & Quality Polish**

## Goal
Comprehensive end-to-end testing and final polish before v1.0.0 release.

## Rationale
Individual components may work but integration can still fail. This phase verifies the complete user journey: installation, first run, configuration, sync, error recovery.

## Test Scenarios
- Fresh install on clean system
- gh CLI detection and auth flow
- First run with no config (should trigger init)
- Sync with public repos (no auth needed)
- Sync with private repos (auth required)
- Handling of dirty repos, diverged repos
- Non-interactive mode in CI
- JSON output parsing with jq

## Polish Items
- Consistent terminology throughout
- Error messages are actionable
- Help text is accurate and complete
- No ShellCheck warnings at severity warning+

## Success Criteria
- All test scenarios pass
- Real user can install and use without reading source code
- Tool feels professional and trustworthy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["polish","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.627181743-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.655784427-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.685174964-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.713971583-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1501","title":"Test fresh installation on clean system","description":"**End-to-end installation test**

## What
Test the complete installation flow on a clean system.

## Why
The installer is users' first experience. It must work flawlessly.

## Test Steps
1. Start with fresh system (Docker or VM)
2. Run curl-bash installer
3. Verify ru is installed and executable
4. Verify --help works
5. Verify --version matches expected

## Environment
Test on:
- Ubuntu latest
- macOS latest

## Acceptance Criteria
- Installer works on clean systems
- ru is in PATH or instructions provided
- No errors during install","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1501","depends_on_id":"bd-15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.037444482-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1501","depends_on_id":"bd-1206","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.069064851-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -1346,8 +1346,103 @@ cmd_sync() {
 }
 
 cmd_status() {
-    log_info "status command not yet implemented"
-    exit 0
+    # Ensure config exists
+    if [[ ! -d "$RU_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
+        log_info "No configuration found. Run: ru init"
+        exit 0
+    fi
+
+    # Check for configured repos
+    local repos_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d/repos.txt"
+    if [[ ! -f "$repos_file" ]] || [[ ! -s "$repos_file" ]] || ! grep -qv '^\s*#\|^\s*$' "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null; then
+        log_info "No repositories configured."
+        log_info "Add repos with: ru add owner/repo"
+        exit 0
+    fi
+
+    # Load all repos from config
+    local repos=()
+    while IFS= read -r line; do
+        [[ -n "$line" ]] && repos+=("$line")
+    done < <(get_all_repos)
+
+    local total=${#repos[@]}
+
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
+        # JSON output mode
+        echo "["
+        local first="true"
+        for repo_spec in "${repos[@]}"; do
+            local url branch custom_name local_path repo_name
+            parse_repo_spec "$repo_spec" url branch custom_name
+            if [[ -n "$custom_name" ]]; then
+                local_path="${PROJECTS_DIR}/${custom_name}"
+            else
+                local_path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
+            fi
+            repo_name=$(basename "$local_path")
+            local status="missing" ahead=0 behind=0 dirty="false" branch_name=""
+            if [[ -d "$local_path" ]] && is_git_repo "$local_path"; then
+                local status_info
+                status_info=$(get_repo_status "$local_path" "$FETCH_REMOTES")
+                status=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*STATUS=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                ahead=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*AHEAD=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                behind=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*BEHIND=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                dirty=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*DIRTY=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                branch_name=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*BRANCH=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+            elif [[ -d "$local_path" ]]; then
+                status="not_git"
+            fi
+            [[ "$first" == "true" ]] || echo ","
+            first="false"
+            printf '{"repo":"%s","path":"%s","status":"%s","branch":"%s","ahead":%d,"behind":%d,"dirty":%s}' \
+                "$repo_name" "$local_path" "$status" "$branch_name" "$ahead" "$behind" "$dirty"
+        done
+        echo "]"
+    else
+        # Human-readable output
+        log_info "Repository Status ($total repos)"
+        [[ "$FETCH_REMOTES" == "true" ]] && log_info "Fetching remotes for accurate status..."
+        echo "" >&2
+        printf "%-30s %-12s %-15s %s\n" "Repository" "Status" "Branch" "Ahead/Behind" >&2
+        printf "%-30s %-12s %-15s %s\n" "------------------------------" "------------" "---------------" "------------" >&2
+        for repo_spec in "${repos[@]}"; do
+            local url branch custom_name local_path repo_name
+            parse_repo_spec "$repo_spec" url branch custom_name
+            if [[ -n "$custom_name" ]]; then
+                local_path="${PROJECTS_DIR}/${custom_name}"
+            else
+                local_path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
+            fi
+            repo_name=$(basename "$local_path")
+            local status="missing" ahead=0 behind=0 dirty="false" branch_name="" status_display
+            if [[ -d "$local_path" ]] && is_git_repo "$local_path"; then
+                local status_info
+                status_info=$(get_repo_status "$local_path" "$FETCH_REMOTES")
+                status=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*STATUS=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                ahead=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*AHEAD=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                behind=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*BEHIND=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                dirty=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*DIRTY=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+                branch_name=$(echo "$status_info" | sed 's/.*BRANCH=\([^ ]*\).*/\1/')
+            elif [[ -d "$local_path" ]]; then
+                status="not_git"
+            fi
+            case "$status" in
+                current)     status_display="${GREEN}current${RESET}" ;;
+                behind)      status_display="${YELLOW}behind${RESET}" ;;
+                ahead)       status_display="${CYAN}ahead${RESET}" ;;
+                diverged)    status_display="${RED}diverged${RESET}" ;;
+                missing)     status_display="${DIM}missing${RESET}" ;;
+                not_git)     status_display="${RED}not_git${RESET}" ;;
+                no_upstream) status_display="${YELLOW}no_upstrm${RESET}" ;;
+                *)           status_display="$status" ;;
+            esac
+            [[ "$dirty" == "true" ]] && status_display="${status_display}${YELLOW}*${RESET}"
+            printf "%-30s %-12b %-15s %d/%d\n" "${repo_name:0:30}" "$status_display" "${branch_name:0:15}" "$ahead" "$behind" >&2
+        done
+        echo "" >&2
+        log_info "Legend: * = uncommitted changes"
+    fi
 }
 
 cmd_init() {
@@ -1394,8 +1489,8 @@ cmd_add() {
             continue
         fi
 
-        # Check if already in file
-        if grep -qF "$repo" "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null; then
+        # Check if already in file (only non-comment lines)
+        if grep -v '^[[:space:]]*#' "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qxF "$repo"; then
             log_warn "Already configured: $repo"
             continue
         fi
